title: Authentication, Authorization, RBAC, Service Accounts, Tokens, and Impersonation · API Object Lens: K8s Rbac Escalation Aggregation
summary: A CI workload uses the default service account and a long-lived token while a broad ClusterRoleBinding permits secret reads and workload creation across tenants. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s rbac escalation aggregation increment in least-privilege identity and authorization policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Detect bind, escalate, impersonate, service account, secret, workload-creation, node-proxy, CSR, aggregation, and wildcard privilege escalation paths.
Construct namespace-scoped Roles and RoleBindings or justified ClusterRoles and ClusterRoleBindings from verbs, resources, names, non-resource URLs, and subjects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example workload uses the default ServiceAccount, automounts a broad token, and receives wildcard cluster-admin through a group binding. Derive subject, verb, resource, namespace, token audience, impersonation, and least-privilege proof. Competency probe: Detect bind, escalate, impersonate, service account, secret, workload-creation, node-proxy, CSR, aggregation, and wildcard privilege escalation paths. content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-identity-rbac-serviceaccounts-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-identity-rbac-serviceaccounts-3
    artifact: rbac-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s identity rbac serviceaccounts 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      serviceAccount: intake/status-api
      automountServiceAccountToken: false
      token: projected-audience-intake-api
      role: get-configmap-status-only
      binding: namespace-intake
      review: auth-can-i-and-escalation-map
    verify: kubectl auth can-i --list --as system:serviceaccount:intake:status-api -n intake
    failure: Reject broad ClusterRoleBinding, long-lived static tokens, wildcard verbs, secret reads, bind or escalate paths, or workload creation as harmless.
    repair: Separate human and workload identity, scope verbs and resources, use bound audience tokens, test authorization, and review escalation periodically.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Read-only access to Pods and Secrets cannot lead to stronger credentials or code execution. This fails the K8s Rbac Escalation Aggregation contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
